rationale: Attempt 1 broken (Qwen logic bug); attempt 2 fully functional. TuringOS substrate (welcome wizard, spec grill, CAS, capsule CID, sandbox) all green. Single-shot Qwen3-Coder reliability is the weak link.

attempt_1.fatal_flaw: init() pre-populates player.matrix; Space-handler guard 'if (player.matrix === null)' is therefore never true on first press, so update() rAF loop is never started. Game frozen on initial render.

attempt_2.t3_score_visible: PASS (分数 / 最高分 / score literals)
attempt_2.t7_no_console_errors: PASS (only Chrome extension noise at :0:0, no game-origin errors)
attempt_2.t8_plays_through: PASS (30 random keystrokes; no panic; piece migrated from posY=0 to posY=14; game still alive)

tape_cas_integrity.agent_audit_trail_jsonl: NOT PRESENT under workspace root (Phase 6.3 uses spec_transcript.jsonl per session instead; not a bug, but architect-spec assumption was wrong)
tape_cas_integrity.turingos_welcome_independent_check: init/llm/agent all confirmed green; spec/generate not detected (workspace-level checker doesn't introspect sessions/, this is a Phase 6.3 reporting gap)

api_key_invariant: HELD — no sk-bokl prefix found anywhere on disk (workspace, tmp, live log all clean)
